title: Shared folder mounts randomly get unmounted
body:
### Issue Description

I'm running Podman on a Mac, and the Podman machine VM randomly loses its shared folder mounts. Here's an example where my VM had been up for a while, and I tried to run a container that uses a bind mount from the "host" (my Mac) and it failed: 

```
make jobs prow-config
false || podman pull --platform linux/amd64 registry.ci.openshift.org/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest
Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:09926637c7bf9825d7afb25218a2b16128eea30d547297bd73d6e53c07025396
Copying blob sha256:8ed15414cb45b8b895a21f106743ceee45272cee0d9e7e5b1f49bd1e877e4a11
Copying config sha256:0cfba2e738cf9bbc204bda5d586d0ed4ae8dfae05826f79b6b140e5d94f91fea
Writing manifest to image destination
0cfba2e738cf9bbc204bda5d586d0ed4ae8dfae05826f79b6b140e5d94f91fea
podman run --platform linux/amd64  --rm -v "/Users/angoldst/code/openshift/release/ci-operator/config:/ci-operator/config" -v "/Users/angoldst/code/openshift/release/ci-operator/step-registry:/ci-operator/step-registry" registry.ci.openshift.org/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest --config-dir /ci-operator/config --registry /ci-operator/step-registry
Error: statfs /Users/angoldst/code/openshift/release/ci-operator/config: no such file or directory
make: *** [ci-operator-checkconfig] Error 125
```

Logging in to the VM indeed shows the mount is missing:

```
podman machine ssh 
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 38.20230918.3.2
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Tue Oct 17 12:04:33 2023 from 192.168.127.1
[core@localhost ~]$ df
Filesystem     1K-blocks     Used Available Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs            4096        0      4096   0% /dev
tmpfs            4058768      168   4058600   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs            1623508     8880   1614628   1% /run
/dev/vda4      104266732 14156312  90110420  14% /sysroot
tmpfs            4058772        0   4058772   0% /tmp
/dev/vda3         358271   206468    128047  62% /boot
tmpfs             811752      164    811588   1% /run/user/501
```

Restarting the VM results in the missing mount returning:

```
podman machine stop && podman machine start
Waiting for VM to exit...
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users:/Users
Mounting volume... /private:/private
Mounting volume... /var/folders:/var/folders

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/angoldst/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it run the\nfollowing commands:

        sudo /usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.7.1/bin/podman-mac-helper install
        podman machine stop/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.7.1/bin/podman-mac-helper; podman machine start/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.7.1/bin/podman-mac-helper

                You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

        export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/angoldst/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
(⎈|default/api-ci-l2s4-p1-openshiftapps-com:6443/angoldst:default) angoldst@angoldst-mac  ~/code/openshift/release   rukpak-z-e2e ±  podman machine ssh                          
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 38.20230918.3.2
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Wed Oct 18 09:56:58 2023 from 192.168.127.1
df[core@localhost ~]$ df
Filesystem     1K-blocks      Used Available Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs            4096         0      4096   0% /dev
tmpfs            4058768        84   4058684   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs            1623508      8888   1614620   1% /run
/dev/vda4      104266732  14221980  90044752  14% /sysroot
tmpfs            4058772         0   4058772   0% /tmp
/dev/vda3         358271    206468    128047  62% /boot
tmpfs             811752         8    811744   1% /run/user/501
vol0           488245288 415811556  72433732  86% /Users
vol1           488245288 415811556  72433732  86% /private
vol2           488245288 415811556  72433732  86% /var/folders
```

In this case, it's **vol0**.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. podman machine start
2. wait some amount of time (?)


### Describe the results you received

Shared folder mount disappears

### Describe the results you expected

Not that :smi

### podman info output

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Using the stable ostree `rpm-ostree rebase fedora/x86_64/coreos/stable`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20122 and https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15976

I assume your VM rebooted automatically due an upgrade. Given the above this should no longer be the case with VM's created with future versions.
